meta.description: Personal health and training database — Todd Blackhurst AI coaching system

oura_biology_baselines.notes: Oura Biology personal baselines. VO2 Max 38.9 (Below Average — cardio fitness improvement goal). HRV baseline 32.5 ms (any reading below this signals recovery deficit). Avg sleep 6h 25m/night — chronic mild sleep debt.

nutrition_targets.notes: Targets set 2026-04-11. ~380 kcal/day deficit (between mild and moderate). Protein elevated for anabolic resistance at age 57. Fat raised for hormone support. Recalculate if weight changes ±3 kg or training frequency shifts.

body_composition.notes: Baseline from Bevel 30-day Progress Report (Nov 5 – Nov 24)
End-point from Bevel 30-day Progress Report — marked as Low on fat distribution
Bevel weekly scan (week of Apr 6). Visceral Fat Level 2.0. BF% 16.2%, weight 75.9 kg / 167.3 lbs. Primary VAT reduction target ongoing.

Hume Body Pod full scan, updated Apr 10. Air-displacement plethysmography (higher BF% than BIA/Bevel). Trunk muscle Low — core/anti-rotation priority confirmed. Bilateral arm fat High. Goal weight 165 lbs.

Bevel morning scan Apr 11, 4:29 AM. Weight stable at 166.0 lbs. Body fat 17.4% — excellent control. Lean mass 137.1 lbs (78.1% muscle) — preserved well. Visceral fat Lv 3.0 indicates modest VAT. BMR 1779 kcal baseline for deficit calculations.

Bevel morning scan Apr 12, 6:47 AM. Weight 165.8 lbs — down 0.2 lbs from Apr 11. BF 16.3% stable. Lean mass 138.8 lbs (79.1%). Visceral Fat Lv 2.0 (down from 3.0 on Apr 11 — positive signal). BMR 1779 kcal consistent. Trending toward 165 lb goal.

body_composition.body_fat_note: BIA underestimate — not reliable for body fat tracking; use Bevel and Hume ADP pod scan as primary reference
body_composition.notes: Hume Pod BIA daily scale Apr 12, 6:51 AM. Weight 166.8 lbs. Metabolic Age 42 yrs (Excellent). Health Score 673. Trunk muscle Low (consistent with ADP scan Apr 10). BF 10.2% is a BIA artifact — ADP scan (Apr 10) measured 22.0%. Segmental swings vs Apr 10 are hydration-driven, not real muscle change. Trunk Low rating structurally persistent.

recovery_sleep.notes: Low strain day — Oura. Productive Sleep Debt Recovery noted. Outdoor cycling session same morning.
Sleep Quality 55%, below average. Time in Bed 7h 43m, Asleep 7h 17m. Wind-down 9:20 PM, target bedtime 9:50 PM.
Recovery 58% Normal. Sleep 73% Above normal. Deep Sleep 59m (notable). HR Dip 14%. Sleep Bank -1h 49m. Cardio Load 29 Detraining. Sleep HRV avg 31ms / max 126ms. Lowest HR 56 bpm.
Significant HRV Drop — Recovery crashed to 26% (HRV 19.3 ms, ~20ms below baseline). Despite 1h 6min deep sleep. Sleep started Apr 3 at 10:19 PM. Reduction in training intensity indicated.
Recovery Breakthrough — HRV surged to 51.4 ms (+52% rebound from Apr 4's 26%). Nervous system adapting well to training. Sleep 5h 50m (started Apr 4 at 10:56 PM). Sleep Score 69 FAIR.

Recovery Slide 41% — HRV 36.2 ms (Recovery metric), 25 ms avg overnight. HRV/HR spike after Apr 8 high-strain session. Sleep Score 69 FAIR. 5h 28m asleep / 7h 4m in bed (9:21 PM – 4:25 AM). Efficiency 77%. Deep 38m, REM 65m. HR Dip 15%. Debt -2h 13m.

Resilience Signal — steady 57% recovery despite Apr 10 high-strain session. HR dropped 3.1 bpm. Rise: 6h 48m sleep / 8h 13m in bed (9:18 PM – 5:31 AM), Efficiency 83%, Deep 43m, REM 1h 18m, Latency 22m, Restfulness Good, Timing Optimal, Debt -2h 30m Moderate. AutoSleep: 7h 14m total, REM 2h 6m, Deep 30m, Debt -2h 8m, Sleep Time 9:38 PM. HR Dip 14%. Lowest HR 58. HRV avg 26ms / max 151ms. Avg HR 64. SpO2 95.2%.

Recovery Breakthrough — Oura 68% (Above normal, +12% from yesterday). Rise sleep score 70/GOOD. REM above normal at 2h 28m (Oura) / 1h 2m (Rise). HRV 41.7ms above personal baseline of 32.5ms. Sleep short at 6h 15m–6h 54m. Moderate sleep debt (~2h 12m). Latency 37m — attributed to elevated metabolism from prior workout. Restfulness flagged. Respiratory rate slightly below normal (14.1 rpm) — monitor. SpO2 96.1% normal. Training guidance: Amber-Green, full session OK, HR <122 bpm. | Motra supplement: HRV Stabilizing (7d +1.6ms, 30d -3.8ms), RHR 62.7 bpm Stable, VO2 Max 39.6 Good (+4.9 over 90d), Weight 166.3 lbs (30d high, +0.8 lbs/wk trend). Training status: Amber-Green confirmed.
recovery_sleep.motra_weight_trend: Increasing — 30-day new high; +0.8 lbs/wk trend; 30-day projection 169.3 lbs

meals.notes: Sugar gap. The peanut M&M's are the signature sweets pattern — 17g carbs, 8g fat, 3g protein. Swap: 1 oz beef jerky next time — same pocket snack, 10g protein, 0g fat, 0g carbs.

meals.notes: Best macro decision of the day — lean protein + rice carbs, minimal fat. Sashimi (6 slices) did most of the protein work at 20g. Roll added the carbs efficiently. Good instinct given the fat overrun earlier.

daily_totals.note: DAY CLOSED. Final: 1954 kcal / 116g P / 130g C / 113g F. Deficit -46 kcal. Protein short -34g. Fat over +43g — walnuts breakfast + ranch lunch. Dinner sushi/sashimi was strong recovery.

nutrition.notes: 5 entries logged via Cal AI. Fat target significantly blown (113g vs 70g target, +43g) — damage concentrated at breakfast (walnuts +26g) and lunch (ranch +39g). Dinner (sushi + sashimi) was a strong recovery choice: 31g protein, 54g carbs, only 8g fat. M&M snack at 4:15 PM was the sweets pattern showing up — 8g fat, 17g sugar. Day calories nearly on target at 1954/2000. Protein 34g short — one more whey shake would close it. Morning walk: 2.22 mi, 198 active cal, avg HR 101 bpm.

High strain score (78%) despite moderate activity — short active window. Cardio load detraining zone.
Volume Spike flagged by Oura — nearly doubled 14-day avg for a single session. High chest and hinge day. HR stayed mostly in lower aerobic zones.

Volume Spike — nearly doubled 14-day average for single session. Warmup block included Cervical CARS, Thoracic Open Book, Glute Bridge, Band Pull Apart, Ext Shoulder Rotation, Kettlebell Swings. HR stayed mostly Z1-Z2. High chest and hinge focus.
Productive Shape Session — 7,764 kg volume. Cardio stayed within HRV limits. Added 13 pts to Cardio Load. Well-executed session.

High-volume posterior chain day. All HR within Zone 1-2, well within <122 bpm constraint. Oura daily recovery 41%.

Morning walk in Beitun District. Avg HR 101 bpm — well within <122 bpm constraint. Easy effort, Zone 1. Good morning activation / low-intensity fat-burning cardio. Supports VAT reduction goal without adding training load.

coaching_relevance.hip_offset: Right-side dominance confirmed — unilateral work and hip alignment cues critical
coaching_relevance.head_deviation: Forward head posture / cervical alignment deficit — cervical CARS and wall slides priority
coaching_relevance.spine_deviation: Significant lateral spine deviation (11.4 cm) — anti-rotation and quadratus lumborum work
coaching_relevance.right_arm_low_score: Right shoulder asymmetry — matches structural correction system priorities
coaching_relevance.flagged_muscles_summary: Posterior chain (traps, infraspinatus, lats, erectors) and anterior chain (serratus, oblique) imbalances both present

posture_assessment.notes: WorldGym Taiwan AI posture scan (Crosspoint system). Dynamic score 77/100. Hip offset -4.3 cm (right dominant). Spine deviation 11.4 cm is significant. Right arm 45 vs left arm 58 confirms shoulder asymmetry. Directly validates structural correction priorities in 20_STRUCTURAL_CORRECTION_PRIORITY_USER.md.

coaching_brief.note: Auto-generated 20:08 run. No new uploads — all files already processed today. Nutrition day closed (5 entries, 1954 kcal). Morning walk completed. Training call updated for 8 PM window.

recovery: 🟡 AMBER-GREEN — 3rd consecutive above-baseline HRV day: Apr 9 (36.2 ms) → Apr 11 (38.8 ms) → Apr 12 (41.7 ms). That streak is rare. Both prior streaks ended after sessions over 70 minutes.
HRV 41.7 ms vs 32.5 ms baseline (+9.2 ms above). Recovery score 68%, +12% from yesterday. Motra confirms: HRV stabilizing, 7d trend +1.6 ms. The system is building.
Sleep: 6h 54m total, debt ~2h 12m. REM above normal (2h 28m Oura) — that's what drove the breakthrough. Latency 37 min and restfulness flagged — fragmented but productive night.
Mild persistent flag: Respiratory rate 14.1 rpm (below normal). SpO2 96.1% normal. Not alarming — flag if it shows a third consecutive day.
training_direction: Tomorrow morning. It's 8:08 PM — a 65-min session ends at 9:13 PM. Your target bedtime is 9:30 PM. That's 17 minutes from session end to sleep onset. You'd blow the recovery streak to get one session in.
If you're already out the door or at the gym — go. Pull-Dominant Upper is the call. Otherwise, lock tomorrow 7–8 AM and protect tonight's sleep.

Rotation: Pull-Dominant Upper + Structural Correction. Last upper was Apr 8 push-heavy (chest 15%). Pull tomorrow balances it: lat pulldown/assisted pull-up → single-arm row (left leads) → face pull → half-kneeling single-arm pulldown (left leads) → Pallof + suitcase carry.
65-minute cap — non-negotiable. 3 of 3 strength sessions exceeded it: Apr 6 (96 min), Apr 8 (118 min), Apr 10 (77 min). Set the timer before your first foam roller rep.
HR: Under 122 bpm. Pull sessions trend lower HR — that's the advantage. Still check before each compound set.
nutrition: Day closed ✅ — 1954 kcal / 116g P / 130g C / 113g F. Calories nearly perfect (-46 kcal). Fat overrun +43g. Protein short 34g. Day is done — no late shake needed.
Root cause: walnuts at breakfast (+26g fat) + ranch at lunch (+24g fat) = 50g of fat before 1 PM. Dinner was smart recovery: sashimi + roll = 31g P / 8g fat.
One structural fix for tomorrow: replace walnuts with cottage cheese (½ cup = 14g protein, 2g fat, same satiety). That single swap brings breakfast fat from 53g to 29g and opens protein room all day.
M&M's at 4:15 PM — swap is beef jerky (same pocket snack, 10g protein, 0g fat). Noted, moving on.
Tomorrow's binding constraint will be protein. Lead every meal with protein — shake before food at breakfast.
progress: 165.8 lbs on Bevel this morning — 0.8 lbs from the 165 lb goal. Down 1.5 lbs since April 6. You're essentially there.
Body fat 16.3% (Bevel), lean mass 138.8 lbs, visceral fat level 2.0 (down from 3.0 on Apr 11). VF dropping without losing lean mass — target outcome confirmed.
VO2 Max 39.6 ml/kg/min (Motra) — highest reading in the database, +4.9 over 90 days. Fasted morning walks are the engine. Keep them.

Motra weight trend shows 166.3 lbs with +0.8 lbs/wk projection — conflicts with Bevel's 165.8 downward trend. Divergence is hydration artifact from Motra's rolling average. Trust Bevel for body comp tracking.
Key risk: Breakfast fat pattern is repeating and structural. Fix it once — the rest of the day self-corrects.
watch_for: 1. If you haven't left for the gym — don't go tonight. Bedtime 9:30 PM protects the HRV streak. Set tomorrow's alarm for 6 AM, gym by 7 AM.
2. Tomorrow breakfast: cottage cheese instead of walnuts. Log it before leaving the house. Two zero-log mornings (Apr 11) broke the tracking streak — 5 entries today was the right recovery.
3. Tomorrow's session: set a 65-min timer before your first prep movement. Your overrun pattern (Apr 6, 8, 10) happens inside the session — the timer is the only reliable fix.

session_plan.session_goal: Build left-side pulling strength, counter right shoulder dominance, integrate thoracic and scapular correction.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Thoracic Extension
exercises.note: Thoracic stiffness is your #3 structural issue. 3 segments — mid-back, upper-mid, upper thoracic. Hold 2 sec at each segment. Sets the scapular baseline for the entire pull session.

exercises.name: Quadruped Thoracic Rotation
exercises.note: Left side first. Right-side dominance restricts left thoracic rotation. Hand behind head, rotate elbow toward ceiling. Controlled descent.

exercises.name: Banded Hip Abduction
exercises.note: Left side leads. Activates glute medius — left hip is the structural weak side per scan. Keep pelvis level, no hip hike.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Aparts
exercises.note: Scapular control primer before pulling. Retract hard at end range, hold 1 second. Reduces upper trap compensation on lat pulldown and rows.

exercises.name: Dead Hang
exercises.note: Shoulder decompression + grip primer. Passive hang, shoulders relaxed into elevation, then active pack. Sets the pull path for the entire session. Skip if shoulder is irritated.

exercises.name: Machine-Assisted Pull-Up
exercises.note: Straight sets. Your Feb 2 log used -80 to -90 kg assist — start at -80 kg and hold if clean. Full ROM: dead hang to chin over bar. Left side will want to bail early — control the descent equally on both sides.
alternatives: Cable Lat Pulldown Wide-Grip (100 kg starting — Feb log)

exercises.name: Single-Arm DB Row (left side first)
exercises.note: Left arm leads — always. Use a weight your left side owns completely, not what the right can handle. Anti-rotation demand is the corrective goal. Brace hard through the planted side.

exercises.name: Cable Face Pull
exercises.note: Rear delt and mid-trap corrective — directly addresses shoulder imbalance (#2 structural issue). Pull to forehead height, elbows flared high, squeeze at end range. Load should allow perfect form every rep. Your Feb log used 20–25 kg.

exercises.name: Half-Kneeling Single-Arm Pulldown (left arm first)
exercises.note: Left arm leads. Half-kneeling position forces anti-rotation core engagement while pulling — stacks two corrections in one movement. Right knee down, pull with left arm. Keep glute of rear leg active throughout.

exercises.name: Low Step-Up (left leg first)
exercises.note: Left leg leads every set. Hip-OA safe — 6–8 inch step max, no deep hip flexion. Drive through heel, full hip extension at top. No push-off from the trailing foot. Slow the descent (2 sec).
alternatives: Supported split squat (rear foot low, left leg forward)

exercises.name: Pallof Press
exercises.note: Anti-rotation. Trunk muscle rated Low on both ADP and BIA scans — this is a priority corrective every session. Press straight out, hold 2 sec, return controlled. Do NOT rotate.

exercises.name: Suitcase Carry (left hand)
exercises.note: Left hand only. Primary right-side dominance corrective. KB or DB. Shoulder packed down, no lateral lean. Walk slow enough to feel the anti-lateral-flexion demand.
alternatives: Overhead carry (left arm, if shoulder stable)

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Slam
exercises.note: Floor slams — not wall. 8 kg ball. Full overhead reach, hard slam, athletic reset. 5 reps only — quality not volume. This is your engagement element: it should feel explosive and satisfying. Check HR before each set.
